name: qdrant-clients-sdk
prompt: I'm writing an ingestion service in Go and need to bulk-load about 5M points into a collection. What's the idiomatic way to do batched upserts with the official Go client, with retries and some parallelism? And is there somewhere I can grab a working sample instead of reverse-engineering it from the REST docs?

rubric.text: Confirms an official Go client exists and gives install/import (go get github.com/qdrant/go-client)
Points to the upsert / upload_points operation for batched ingestion (idempotent on point id)
Surfaces an actual code snippet or the curated snippet-search endpoint, not only prose
Mentions parallel uploads + retry settings (e.g. parallel, max_retries) for robust bulk loading
Notes idempotent overwrite on same id / auto-generated UUIDs when ids omitted
Claims Qdrant has no Go SDK, or sends the user only to the raw REST docs to assemble it themselves
